title: leucas ciliata var. sericostoma
intro: Leucas ciliata var. sericostoma, also known as the hairy leucas, is a plant species belonging to the family Lamiaceae, commonly found in tropical regions. Traditionally, the hairy leucas has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and alleviating skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the hairy leucas can be consumed as a decoction, made by infusing the plant parts in hot water, or used in the form of a paste or powder to treat various ailments.
